title: Ensure no Object Storage buckets are publicly visible.

input_row.title: Ensure no Object Storage buckets are publicly visible.
input_row.description: A bucket is a logical container for storing objects. It is associated with a single compartment that has policies that determine what action a user can perform on a bucket and on all the objects in the bucket. By Default a newly created bucket is private. It is recommended that no bucket be publicly accessible.
input_row.rationale: Removing unfettered reading of objects in a bucket reduces an organization's exposure to data loss.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login into the OCI Console 2. Click in the search bar at the top of the screen. 3. Type Advanced Resource Query and click enter. 4. Click the Advanced Resource Query button in the upper right of the screen. 5. Enter the following query in the query box: query bucket resources where (publicAccessType == 'ObjectRead') || (publicAccessType == 'ObjectReadWithoutList') 6. Ensure query returns no results From CLI: 1. Execute the following command: oci search resource structured-search --query-text "query bucket resources where (publicAccessType == 'ObjectRead') || (publicAccessType == 'ObjectReadWithoutList')" 2. Ensure query returns no results Cloud Guard To Enable Cloud Guard Auditing: Ensure Cloud Guard is enabled in the root compartment of the tenancy. For more information about enabling Cloud Guard, please look at the instructions included in Recommendation 3.15. From Console: 1. Type Cloud Guard into the Search box at the top of the Console. 2. Click Cloud Guard from the “Services” submenu. 3. Click Detector Recipes in the Cloud Guard menu. 4. Click OCI Configuration Detector Recipe (Oracle Managed) under the Recipe Name column. 5. Find Bucket is public in the Detector Rules column. 6. Verify that the Bucket is public Detector Rule is Enabled. From CLI: 1. Verify the Bucket is public Detector Rule in Cloud Guard is enabled to generate Problems if Object Storage Buckets are configured to be accessible over the public Internet with the following command: oci cloud-guard detector-recipe-detector-rule get --detector-recipe-id --detector-rule-id BUCKET_IS_PUBLIC
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Follow the audit procedure above. 2. For each bucket in the returned results, click the Bucket Display Name 3. Click Edit Visibility 4. Select Private 5. Click Save Changes From CLI: 1. Follow the audit procedure 2. For each of the buckets identified, execute the following command: oci os bucket update --bucket-name --public-access-type NoPublicAccess
input_row.impact: For updating an existing bucket, care should be taken to ensure objects in the bucket can be accessed through either IAM policies or pre-authenticated requests.
input_row.justification: The task of ensuring no Object Storage buckets are publicly visible can be automated using AWS APIs. The second AI opinion correctly identifies that AWS S3 buckets can be checked for public access using the `boto3` library or AWS CLI. This approach leverages AWS's capabilities to programmatically list buckets and evaluate their access permissions, making it a suitable candidate for automation.
input_row.automation_summary: - AWS Resource/Service: S3 Buckets - API/CLI Method: `boto3` methods `s3.list_buckets()`, `s3.get_bucket_acl()`, and `s3.get_bucket_policy()` - Properties to Check: Bucket ACLs and policies for public access permissions - Pass Criteria: No public access permissions are found in bucket ACLs or policies - Fail Criteria: Any bucket with public access permissions in its ACL or policy indicates non-compliance

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** oci_object_storage_bucket_public_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **OCI APIs/OCI Python SDK/OCI CLI Used:**
    - OCI CLI: `oci search resource structured-search --query-text "query bucket resources where (publicAccessType == 'ObjectRead') || (publicAccessType == 'ObjectReadWithoutList')"`
    - OCI Python SDK: Use the `oci.object_storage.ObjectStorageClient` to list all buckets in all compartments, then call `get_bucket()` for each and check the `public_access_type` field.
    - Cloud Guard: Optionally, confirm via Cloud Guard Detector Rule (`Bucket is public`) is enabled to ensure continuous detection.

- **Resources Enumerated:**
    - All Object Storage buckets in all compartments of the tenancy.

- **Configuration Properties Validated:**
    - For each bucket, check the value of `public_access_type`.
        - Valid secure value: `NoPublicAccess`
        - Non-compliant values: `ObjectRead`, `ObjectReadWithoutList` (these allow public bucket access)

- **Pass Criteria:**
    - All buckets have `public_access_type` set to `NoPublicAccess`.
    - The structured search query returns an empty result set.

- **Fail Criteria:**
    - Any bucket is found with `public_access_type` of `ObjectRead` or `ObjectReadWithoutList`.
    - The structured search query returns one or more buckets.

- **Example CLI Audit Command:**
    ```
    oci search resource structured-search --query-text "query bucket resources where (publicAccessType == 'ObjectRead') || (publicAccessType == 'ObjectReadWithoutList')"
    ```
    - Review the results for any buckets returned. If none are listed, compliance is achieved.

- **Remediation (if needed, via CLI):**
    ```
    oci os bucket update --bucket-name <bucket_name> --public-access-type NoPublicAccess --compartment-id <compartment_ocid>
    ```

- **References:**
    - [Managing Buckets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Object/Tasks/managingbuckets.htm)

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_Foundations_Benchmark_v3_0_0
